financial.note: A high-dividend play in 2026; recently approved a ฿0.51 cash dividend (Ex-date Mar 6, Payment Apr 30, 2026), yielding ~4.9%.
Maintains a healthy interest coverage ratio (30x+); however, rising construction costs for new buildings in 2026 increase capital expenditure sensitivity.
Maintains a healthy interest coverage ratio (30x+); however, rising construction costs for new buildings in 2026 increase capital expenditure sensitivity.

supply_chain.note: Operates under the Private Schools Act; changes in curriculum standards or international school quotas directly affect licensing and operations.

supply_chain.entity: Construction Firms (e.g., for Pracha Uthit Expansion)
supply_chain.note: SISB Pracha Uthit is currently adding a 6-storey Secondary-Sixth Form block slated for 2026 completion; delivery delays impact enrollment capacity.
SISB is the official iPSLE centre; the 'Singapore Curriculum' brand is their primary competitive moat for attracting Thai and Chinese students.

competitors.note: Primary rival for premium expat and high-net-worth local families.
Competes in the ultra-premium segment; SISB counters with lower tuition fees and a trilingual focus (Thai, English, Chinese).

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Demand for international schooling is a proxy for luxury discretionary spending; sensitivity to local GDP growth.
Foreign direct investment in Thailand (e.g., EEC) drives expat enrollment, particularly in the Rayong campus.
As a service-based business, staff costs (expat teachers) are the largest overhead; wage inflation squeezes operating margins (currently ~35%).
